title: Kaleh Pacheh (Sheep's Head and Hooves Soup)
description: A very traditional, hearty Persian breakfast soup made by slow-cooking sheep's head and hooves overnight, creating a nutrient-dense, rich broth.

instructions.text: Crucial Step: Clean the sheep's head and hooves extremely thoroughly. Rinse repeatedly under cold water, scrubbing carefully to remove any remaining hairs or impurities.
Place the cleaned sheep's head and hooves into a very large, heavy-bottomed stockpot. Add enough water to cover completely.
Bring the water to a rapid boil. Let it boil for 10 minutes, then discard all the water and rinse the meat and pot. This removes impurities and strong odors.
Return the meat to the clean pot. Add the quartered onion, peeled garlic, cinnamon stick, turmeric, and black pepper. Pour in the 12 cups of fresh water.

Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to the absolute lowest setting. Cover tightly and let it simmer overnight (at least 8 to 10 hours) until the meat easily falls off the bones and the broth is rich.
About an hour before finishing, add the salt to taste.
Carefully separate the meat from the bones. Serve the rich broth in bowls, followed by the meat. Season heavily with fresh lime juice at the table to cut the richness.

tips: This dish requires immense patience and meticulous cleaning. The overnight simmer is what extracts the gelatin from the hooves, creating the signature silky broth.
storage_notes: Leftovers can be refrigerated for 3 days. The broth will solidify into a firm jelly when cold due to the collagen. Reheat gently.
serve_with: Fresh Sangak or Lavash bread, raw onions, and extra lime juice.

substitutions.note: If sheep's head is unavailable or unappealing, you can mimic the rich broth using beef marrow bones and shanks, though it won't be authentic Kaleh Pacheh.
Can be used to help neutralize strong odors during cooking.
